what_it_does: Records a posted noticeboard tip that references two image assets and states a relationship between them using a bidirectional arrow.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares id, address, type=tip, source=claude_code, state=posted, title=Reference, author_session=chatgpt-gpt-5.5-thinking
links list contains two references of kind references pointing to image://design-source/generated/keystone-v3-trial and image://design-source/deck1-2026/p-01
body repeats the two image URIs separated by a bidirectional arrow
channel and thread fields are empty strings
created and updated timestamps are identical
history contains a single filed transition from null to posted
standouts: Bidirectional arrow between a generated trial image and a deck slide image
author_session is chatgpt-gpt-5.5-thinking while source is claude_code
empty channel/thread despite being a noticeboard item
title is generic Reference with no explanatory text
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with id, address, type, state, timestamps, history, and links
Uses image:// URI scheme for design asset references
Includes provenance fields source and author_session
Body mirrors the link relationship in plain text
concerns: Empty channel and thread fields reduce discoverability
source and author_session values differ without explanation
Title and body do not explain the semantic meaning of the reference
created and updated are equal even though the item is in posted state
No description of why the two images are related
notes: A bare reference entry that links a generated design trial image to a deck slide image but leaves the relationship semantics unstated.
questions: What does the bidirectional arrow signify between the two images?
Why is source claude_code while author_session is chatgpt-gpt-5.5-thinking?
What are keystone-v3-trial and deck1-2026/p-01 specifically?
Is this a comparison, derivation, dependency, or alternate view?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated as a reference tip; it accurately records a bidirectional reference between two image assets, though the nature of the reference is unspecified.
apparent_themes: Provenance tracking for generated and deck assets
intention_signals: It appears to flag a connection between a generated design trial and a deck slide image so the relationship can be retrieved or compared later.
novelty: The use of a bidirectional arrow in the body as a lightweight, human-readable relationship marker between two image:// assets.
contribution: The explicit cross-reference between two image assets, plus provenance metadata that records who filed it and when.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard tip item that records a bidirectional reference between the image assets keystone-v3-trial and deck1-2026/p-01 with provenance metadata.
intention_for_embedding: To flag a relationship between a generated design trial image and a deck slide image for later retrieval or comparison.
